note_data.title: The internet and its uses
keyPoints: Binary system is used to process data in logic gates/transistors, to store data in registers, and to process data on the computer
Hexadecimal is used in colour codes, error codes and IP & MAC addresses because it is easily understandable, easier to debug, and takes less space on the screen
Overflow error is the result of carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size
Each shift left is equivalent to multiplying the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Each shift right is equivalent to dividing the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Two's complement format is used to represent negative binary numbers, where the leftmost bit is changed to a negative value
A character set is a list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer, with each character and symbol having a unique value
Sampling is used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals to give an approximate representation of the sound wave

definitions.definition: A list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer
The result of carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size
Used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals. This gives an approximate representation of the sound wave
The number of samples taken per second
The number of bits required per sample (i.e: bit depth)

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give detailed characteristics or features of the concept
State similarities and differences between two things

modelAnswer.question: Explain the difference between the internet and the World Wide Web. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: The internet is a worldwide collection of interconnected networks that communicate using TCP/IP protocols. The World Wide Web is a service that runs on the internet. The internet provides the infrastructure for data transmission between computers. The World Wide Web uses this infrastructure to access websites and web pages through hyperlinks and web browsers.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: WWW is a service on the internet
Mark 4: WWW uses browsers/hyperlinks to access websites

commonMistakes: If the leftmost 1 bits are lost while shifting to the left, an error will occur because the limit of the maximum number of left shifts possible will have been exceeded
If the rightmost 1 bits are lost while shifting to the right, an error will occur because the limit of the maximum number of right shifts possible will have been exceeded
Overflow error occurs when the value is greater than the maximum value of the register
examinerTips: Break binary numbers into sets of 4 when converting to hexadecimal
The accuracy of a sound file increases with greater sample rates and sampling resolutions, but so does the file size
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Network diagram showing computers connected through routers and ISPs to illustrate internet infrastructure, with web servers and clients accessing WWW services
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to describe packet switching as a method of transmitting messages across the internet, requiring explanation of data breaking, independent routing, and reconstruction
O/N 2022 P1 Q7 (8 marks) tested TCP/IP protocol suite application when messages are sent through internet, expecting candidates to explain layer communication and message flow direction
M/J 2024 P2 Q3 (5 marks) examined the functions of Transport and Internet layers specifically, with marks awarded for packet creation, sequencing, flow control, and IP addressing
O/N 2023 P1 Q5 (4 marks) required completion of a table comparing modems and dedicated lines for internet transmission, testing understanding of analogue/digital conversion and connection types

workedExample.question: Describe how the TCP/IP protocol suite is applied when a message is sent through the internet. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify that TCP/IP uses a layered stack architecture with multiple protocol layers
Step 2: Explain communication occurs between adjacent layers only, not across multiple layers simultaneously
Step 3: Describe outbound message flow - message passes from top layer to bottom layer when being sent
Step 4: Describe inbound message flow - message passes from bottom layer to top layer when being received
Step 5: Mention specific layer functions - each layer adds/removes headers and performs specific protocols
workedExample.answer: TCP/IP uses a layered protocol stack architecture. Communication occurs only between adjacent layers in the stack. When sending a message, data passes from the top layer (Application) down through each layer to the bottom (Link layer), with each layer adding appropriate headers. When receiving, the message travels from bottom layer up to the top layer, with each layer removing and processing its headers. This layered approach ensures reliable data transmission across the internet.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for layered stack, 1 mark for adjacent layer communication, 2 marks for correct message flow directions (top-to-bottom sending, bottom-to-top receiving), 1 mark for header processing, 1 mark for mentioning reliability/purpose

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused packet switching with circuit switching' — always emphasize that packets travel independently via different routes and are reassembled at destination
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students often omitted the reconstruction phase of packet switching' — always mention that packets must be reassembled in correct order at the receiving end

Examiner Report 2022: 'Weak responses on TCP/IP layers confused the functions of Internet and Transport layers' — remember Transport handles packet creation/sequencing while Internet handles IP addressing/routing
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must draw a TCP/IP stack showing 4 layers (Application, Transport, Internet, Link) with arrows indicating data flow. Show a message entering at Application layer, passing down through each layer with headers being added (encapsulation), then at receiving end show reverse process with headers being removed (de-encapsulation) as data moves up the layers. Include labels showing what each layer adds: Transport adds port numbers, Internet adds IP addresses, Link adds MAC addresses.
note_data.quickRecap: Binary is fundamental for computer processing and data storage. Hexadecimal provides a more compact and readable representation for codes and addresses. Understanding overflow errors and binary shifts is crucial for data manipulation. Two's complement enables negative number representation in binary systems.
